content: <paragraph>We at Misaki (together with our affiliates, "Misaki" or "we") respect your privacy and are firmly committed to properly safeguarding the information we obtain from you or about you. This Privacy Policy explains how we process personal data collected from you or about you when you use our websites, applications, and services (collectively, the "Services").</paragraph><heading>Personal Data We Collect</heading><paragraph>We collect personal data relating to you as follows:</paragraph><paragraph><bold>Personal data you provide:</bold> If you create an account to use our Services or communicate with us, we collect personal data including:</paragraph><unorderedList><listItem>Account information: When you create an account through us, we collect information associated with your account, including your name, contact information, account credentials, date of birth, payment information, and transaction history (collectively, "Account Information").</listItem><listItem>User content: We collect personal data you provide while using our Services ("Content"), including prompts and other content you upload, such as files, images, and audio, depending on the features you use.</listItem><listItem>Communication information: If you communicate with us, such as by email or through our social media pages, we collect personal data such as your name, contact information, and the content of messages you send ("Communication Information").</listItem><listItem>Other information you provide: We collect other information you may provide to us, such as when you participate in our events or surveys, or provide information to verify your identity or age (collectively, "Other Information You Provide").</listItem></unorderedList><paragraph><bold>Personal data we receive during your use of the Services:</bold> When you access, use, or interact with the Services, we receive the following information ("Technical Information"):</paragraph><unorderedList><listItem>Log data: We collect information your browser or device automatically sends while you use the Services. Log data includes your IP address, browser type and settings, the date and time of your request, and how you interact with the Services.</listItem><listItem>Usage data: We collect information about your use of the Services, such as the types of content you view or use, features you use, actions you take, your time zone and country or region, date and time of access, user agent and version, computer or mobile device type, and your computer connection details.</listItem><listItem>Device information: We collect information about the device you use to access the Services, such as device name, operating system, device identifiers, and browser in use. The information collected may depend on your device type and settings.</listItem><listItem>Location information: We may determine the approximate location of the device you use to access the Services based on information such as IP address, in order to improve security, for example by detecting abnormal login activity to protect your account. This also helps us respond more accurately and improve your product experience. In addition, some Services allow you to choose to provide more precise location information, such as GPS data from your device.</listItem><listItem>Cookies and similar technologies: We use cookies and similar technologies to operate and manage the Services and improve your experience. If you use our Services without creating an account, we may use cookies to store certain information described in this Privacy Policy, such as your browsing preferences. For more details about our use of cookies, please read our Cookie Notice.</listItem><listItem>Information we obtain from other sources: We may obtain information from trusted partners, such as security partners, to protect our Services against fraud, abuse, and other security threats. We may also receive information from marketing providers, including information about potential business service customers.</listItem></unorderedList><heading>How We Use Personal Data</heading><paragraph>We may use personal data for the following purposes:</paragraph><unorderedList><listItem>To provide, analyze, and maintain our Services, including responding to your inquiries to Misaki;</listItem><listItem>To improve and develop our Services, and conduct research, including to develop new product features;</listItem><listItem>To communicate with you, including sending information about our Services and activities, such as information about service changes or improvements;</listItem><listItem>To prevent fraud, illegal activity, or abuse of our Services, and to ensure the security of our systems and Services;</listItem><listItem>To comply with legal obligations and protect the rights, privacy, safety, or property of our users, Misaki, or third parties.</listItem></unorderedList><paragraph>We may also aggregate or de-identify personal data so it can no longer identify you and use that information for the purposes above, such as analyzing how our Services are used, improving and adding service features, and conducting research. We will maintain and use de-identified information in de-identified form and will not attempt to re-identify it unless required by law.</paragraph><paragraph>As described above, we may use Content you provide to improve our Services, such as training models that support Misaki. Please review our instructions for how to opt out of using your Content to train our models.</paragraph><heading>Disclosure of Personal Data</heading><paragraph>We may disclose your personal data in the following circumstances or to the following parties:</paragraph><unorderedList><listItem>Vendors and service providers: To help us meet business operation needs and perform certain services and functions, we may disclose your personal data to vendors and service providers, including hosting providers, customer service providers, cloud service providers, content delivery providers, support and security monitoring providers, email communication software providers, web analytics providers, payment and transaction processors, and other information technology providers. These vendors and service providers will access, process, or store your personal data only as instructed by us and only as necessary to perform their duties to us.</listItem><listItem>Business transfers: If we are involved in a strategic transaction, reorganization, bankruptcy, receivership, or transfer of Services to another provider (collectively, a "Transaction"), we may disclose your personal data during due diligence with counterparties and other parties assisting with the Transaction. As part of a Transaction, your personal data may also be transferred along with other assets to successors or affiliates.</listItem><listItem>Government authorities or other third parties: We may share your personal data, including information about your interactions with our Services, with government authorities, industry peers, or other third parties in accordance with law, including in any of the following cases or for any of the following purposes: (i) where sharing is necessary, or reasonably believed necessary, to comply with a legal obligation; (ii) to protect and defend our rights or property; (iii) if we determine that there are violations of our terms, policies, or the law; (iv) to detect or prevent fraud or other illegal activity; (v) to ensure the safety and integrity of our products, employees, users, or the public; or (vi) to protect against legal liability.</listItem><listItem>Affiliates: We may disclose personal data to our affiliates. Affiliates are entities that control Misaki, are controlled by Misaki, or are under common control with Misaki. Our affiliates may use personal data in a manner consistent with this Privacy Policy.</listItem><listItem>Enterprise account administrators: When you join a Misaki enterprise edition or enterprise account, that account's administrators may access and control your Misaki account, including access to your Content. In addition, if you create an account using an email address belonging to your employer or another organization, we may inform your employer or organization that you have an account and provide related account information, such as your email address, so you can be added to the relevant enterprise account.</listItem><listItem>Other users and third parties you interact with or share information with: Certain features allow you to interact with or share information with other users or third parties. For example, you may share Misaki conversations with other users through share links. You may also send information to third-party applications through custom actions, or search the web to help answer questions using up-to-date information. Information you share with third parties is governed by those third parties' terms and privacy policies, and you should make sure you understand them before sharing information.</listItem></unorderedList><heading>Retention of Personal Data</heading><paragraph>We will retain your personal data only for as long as necessary to provide you with the Services or for other legitimate business purposes (for example, resolving disputes, security reasons, or complying with legal obligations). How long we retain personal data depends on several factors, including:</paragraph><paragraph>For example:</paragraph><unorderedList><listItem>The purpose for processing the data (for example, whether we need to retain the data to provide the Services);</listItem><listItem>The amount, nature, and sensitivity of the data;</listItem><listItem>The potential risk of harm from unauthorized use or disclosure of the data;</listItem><listItem>The legal requirements we must comply with.</listItem></unorderedList>
